jpDisassemblyReference.evidence: The sibling Japanese disassembly uses the same BPEJ baserom hash and confirms the JP build contains the standard m4a/sound code paths. Its current map/cfg do not preserve split song/sample labels, so each manifest item is additionally limited to ROM offsets whose bytes match the current named Expansion asset.

songTable.evidence: The table is the only BPEJ structure found with the expected Emerald gSongTable entry pattern: mus_dummy followed by SE entries, valid song pointers, and music player ids 0/1/1.

songs.label: mus_dummy
songs.evidence: BPEJ song table index 0 points to this header; header bytes match the current mus_dummy sequence header and the entry uses MUSIC_PLAYER_BGM.

songs.label: mus_level_up
songs.evidence: BPEJ song table index 367 points to this header; all five BPEJ track byte ranges match the current mus_level_up track bytes exactly, and the source track contains no pointer commands requiring relocation.

songs.label: se_use_item
songs.evidence: BPEJ song table index 1 points to this header; the BPEJ track byte range matches the current se_use_item track bytes exactly, and the source track contains no pointer commands requiring relocation.

blobs.label: DirectSoundWaveData_sc88pro_glockenspiel
blobs.evidence: The existing sc88pro_glockenspiel.bin byte sequence occurs exactly once in BPEJ at this offset.

blobs.label: DirectSoundWaveData_bicycle_bell
blobs.evidence: The existing bicycle_bell.bin byte sequence occurs exactly once in BPEJ at this offset.
